programmes.show_description: This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
Red Meets Blue. Red discovers that she's not the only colour in the world when she meets Blue. Learn which objects can be red and which can be blue, and how some can be both red and blue.
The Shiny Shell. Baba finds a beautiful, shiny shell that causes some seagull trouble for Oona.
PJ Party. Bing is sleeping at Sula's house but has forgotten Hoppity!
Bouncy-Slidey Day. Love Monster has to overcome his fears of the big slide to rescue Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny when she gets scared.
Drum Drama. The Drums Festival approaches, but everyone's drums keep vanishing. The Piggies hide inside a huge drum to try to discover what's going on.
Treasure Hunt. Ginger wakes the Pea-Rexes with a surprise, a breakfast treasure hunt for yummy drops!
Orange. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's the daring tightrope-walking Orange!
UFO. Mojo and Bo only wanted to help fix a satellite dish with Mojo's chopper top. Now they've been mistaken for a UFO, and the whole town is in a tizzy about invading aliens!
The Three Ways of the Track. With guidance from the ever-wise Hanzo, an easily flustered Tai must learn how to remain focused, calm and balanced while on the job.
Carrot Powers Up. Carrot wants to become superstrong, so she restores Evil Pea's robot suit for herself.
Dark. Children's animation. It is bedtime, but where is Hoppity? Bing remembers playing with him in the garden, but now it is dark out there and he doesn't really like the dark.
Magnets. Coco shows Bing how magnets work, but then Bing drops the magnet behind the flowerbed!
The Same Badge. As Peggee and the Hummingbirds journey up the river to deliver a parcel, they have fun spotting and avoiding the wildlife that they meet along the way.
The Days of the Week Badge. After finding out that Mr Wobbly is visiting next Monday, the Squirrels impatiently want to know how long that means they need to wait.
Go Jetter Heroes: Amelia Earhart. Xuli follows in the footsteps of her hero Amelia Earhart in a transatlantic adventure.
The Tale of the Surprising Sisters. Animated series. Flopsy and Mopsy insist on accompanying Peter on one of his adventures.
Octonauts and the Baby Sea Turtles. Animated deep sea adventure series. As newborn baby sea turtles make their way to the ocean, the Octonauts must help them avoid predators.
Sweetwood's Itchy Scratchies. When animal after animal show up at Vida's clinic with a mysterious rash, she's determined to find the cause. It's poison ivy!
Panic at the Pumpkin Patch. Is there really a ghost at Jackson's pumpkin patch? Mojo and Bo need to find out!
Gamer. Can you complete every level of my Gamer Challenge? Level one is runners, two is squats and three is burpees. The boss battle is the Activate!
Colours. Educational series using Makaton sign language. Mr Tumble accidentally paints a rainbow, while Justin and his friends put on a colourful show at a theatre!
Work. In a game of 'Work', Dad hires Bluey and Bingo at his drainpipe factory. But when Bluey takes charge, Dad finds his new boss pretty tough, especially her no dancing rule.
Dragon. Bluey asks Dad to help her draw a dragon for her story.
The Action Hero Badge. The Squirrels learn about an action hero called Jet Rocket who always saves the day by smashing and crashing things. But there are other ways to save the day!
The Training Badge. The Squirrels need some training to improve their concentration.
Pancake's Syncing Feeling. Pancake is worried about spoiling the synchronised swim team's contest hopes, because of his two left flippers.
The Day We Landed. Cosima and Dad crash-land on an unexplored planet, Proxima B, and meet Big Lizard!
Bubble Trouble. Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes go on an underwater adventure inside a rubbery bubble.
Tulip Fields, Netherlands. Flower power! Foz and Xuli race to stop a runaway harvester destroying a tulip field.
Mission Calm. The ducklings transfer Weasy's mind into the body of a snail - what could go wrong?
Ock's Obey Ray/Lemur at Large. Hulk obeys Doc Ock. A small lemur makes big trouble.
Orangutans. While cleaning up a fire, the Octonauts encounter some fast-learning orangutans.
Leaves. Myrtle's house is full of leaves after an accident with a leaf blower. What will Stan do?
Tato Tower Takeover. Evil Pea and the Mini Peas take over the Tato Tower HQ and lock Supertato out.

programmes.show_name: New: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programmes.show_description: Ginger Finds Out. Ginger discovers the Peak City Heroes' big secret.
Moon Baby. Animated series about a group of singing potatoes. In this soft lullaby, Moon Baby observes the galaxy from up on the moon before floating back home to his cosy nursery.
Time Machine. Rebecca and Murphi are planning to go on a travelling adventure, so they use straws to make a sailboat. They also create a paper plate dinosaur to keep them company!
Isaac the Inventor. Jen makes a rain stick using rice, and Small wants one too. Isaac the inventor visits.
Silly Faces and Picnic Walk. Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers explore a market and make silly faces on a walk to a picnic.
Giant Chinese Salamander and Alaotran Gentle Lemur. Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet Chinese giant salamanders and Alaotran gentle lemurs.
Waffle Brings Us All Together. Mrs Hobbs challenges the After Schoolers to vote for their favourite activity - and to do it again on Mr Brooklyn-Bell's arrival.
The Tidy Up Badge. Animated preschool series. It is time for a Squirrel clubhouse spring clean. Duggee is dusting when the doorbell goes and a big package arrives for him.
Manatees. Animated deep sea adventures with Captain Barnacles and his explorers. Running out of air, Barnacles struggles to free his paw from the mouth of a giant clam.
Pippen's Vanishing Voice. Pippen loses her voice before her concert. Can Vida help her get it back for her solo?
The Grand Canyon, USA. Animated adventure comedy series. Kyan is wire-walking across the Grand Canyon, but Glitch wants to cheat and win. Can the Go Jetters stop him?
The Switch Places Wish. Rafi wishes she could be a grown-up for a while, and Aunty T wishes she could play in the park. A switching wish lets them swap places.
Water Music. Sonny organises a tea party in his caravan to show off his new musical instrument, but rain comes pouring through the leaky roof. Are his plans for a musical afternoon a washout?
Asparagus. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's young Asparagus, sharing his deep affection for wet weather in the song I Love the Rain.
A Big Concert! George from the CBeebies House helps Justin with a big concert.
Making Sounds. Toddlers and their grown-ups explore making sounds, play, sing songs and share a story.
Mother Earth. Lou is saved by Kiri from a nightmare in which the Earth seems to have a mind of its own.
Bed Bother. The Piggies make Rebecca a new bed, but Wilf falls asleep on it.
The Sweetwood Explorer's Helping Day. Grandpa Beaver has a leak in his dam, and it's up to the Sweetwood Explorers to help him!
Leaves. Myrtle's house is full of leaves after an accident with a leaf blower. What will Stan do?
An Applephant for Boxy. The Game Catchers land on a planet with rivers and lakes made of fruit juice, and learn to play hoop game.
Playtime. The Jungle Park is full of baby Vegesaurs. Ginger and the Pea-Rexes invent a game for everyone to play.
Waffle the Cheerleader. Mrs Hobbs's tidy up activity turns into cheerleading chaos when Waffle runs off with a pompom. Once the hall is clean, Evie leads a cheerleading finale.
Waking Up. Aaron, Novah and Joda go on a springtime adventure, meeting a beatboxing daffodil to find out why all the plants are starting to wake up.
Super Like You. The Rakoonz are inspired by a comic to defeat the Peak City Heroes with a giant robot.   Followed by Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest.
Andy and the Runaway Ankylosaurus. Andy and Jen embark on an epic adventure across Dino Island with a runaway Ankylosaurus.
Disco Fog. Evil Pea fogs up the supermarket by using a fog machine so that Supertato and the Tato Team get lost forever - but she ends up getting lost herself.
Wouldn't It Be Loverly. Pink Bird dons a pair of dancing shoes in the Magic Hands rendition of Wouldn't It Be Loverly from My Fair Lady, performed by Mae and Aimee in British Sign Language.
Nigel Had a Long Neck. Nick and the Popcasters create a song about Nigel the Giraffe, who longs to see the view from the top of Primrose Hill.
Juno's Long-Lasting Cast. Juno struggles to be a calm patient while she waits for Vida to remove her cast.
All About Home. Cosima is so excited to tell Big Lizard about her life on earth that she doesn't realise he has something exciting to say to her.
The Unicorn Wish. Rafi's unicorn wish brings exciting quests, but she realises even heroes need rest - and the best adventures can be found at home.
Queens. During a game of queens, Bluey and Bingo find that playing the butler is much more fun. But someone must play the queen, even if it means not being afraid of a bit of hard work.
It's Time to Find a Butterfly. JoJo searches for a butterfly to complete her nature tick-book. She learns that all butterflies start life as furry little caterpillars.
World Bread Day. Animation. When there's a bread festival in the park, Sarah and Duck find themselves part of an exciting treasure hunt.
It Is Absolutely Completely Not Messy. Children's animation with the siblings. Lola won't tidy up the bedroom, and Charlie can't persuade her that a tidy room is a good thing.
Where Are the Friends? Everybody is very surprised to discover the Roundabout has been left in the sitting room.
Good to Share. BaBa enjoys sharing with friends but is disappointed when they are left with nothing. Luckily, MeMo and BaBa's other kind friends enjoy sharing back.
Washing the Haahoos. Children's series. Makka Pakka washes the smallest Pontipines' and the biggest Haahoos' faces. The Tombliboos have fun cleaning their teeth.
Katherine and Nat Sciver-Brunt - Find Your Happy. England cricketers Katherine and Nat Sciver-Brunt read a Bedtime Story about finding happiness.
This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
Pumpkin. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Pumpkin demonstrating incredible strength.
Stuck. Children's animation. Bing and Sula clamber around on the trees in the park. At first, Sula needs Bing's help to climb up, but then Bing climbs a bit too high.
Lights Out. Preschool series. When the lights go out in the Home Dome, the Teletubbies get the giggles! The lights come back on again and there is Tubby Custard mess everywhere.
Worm. Tee and his friends find a worm and build it a house to live in. But is this really the perfect home for a worm?
Broccoli Alone. Supertato is out chasing a milkshake robot and leaves Broccoli in charge of Tato Tower. Evil Pea tries to take over the tower, but Broccoli puts on a great defence.
Birthday. It's Bing's third birthday, but there's trouble when he teaches his friends the waka-oke.
Mojo Drop the Ball. Mojo and Bo's wrecking ball might destroy a lot more than it's supposed to!
Night and Day. What happens at the Clubhouse during night and day? Delivery Chipmunk delivers a parcel to Hennie, the chickens feel seasick and Hatsu describes everything she likes.
The Puzzle Badge. The Squirrels are close to finishing a jigsaw puzzle.
Cricket. During a friendly game of neighbourhood cricket, the dads struggle to bowl Rusty out.
Flat-Pack. While Mum and Dad struggle to build new flat-pack furniture, Bluey and Bingo transform the empty boxes into their own new world.
Mars Mission, Chile. Xuli, Foz and Ubercorn help Nasa test their Mars Rover, until a close encounter with Glitch's UFO causes it to run out of control.
The Tale of Peter's Great Escape. Animated series. When the rabbits find themselves surrounded, Peter is forced to create a brand new ingenious escape route.
The Giant Squid. Animated deep sea adventures with Captain Barnacles and his band of explorers. The Octonauts set out to find and photograph the elusive giant squid.
Six. Children's animation. The Numberblocks make a new friend who likes to roll dice. Learn all about the number 6 with Numberblock Six.
Wig. The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. O meets a wig and thinks it is the cutest pet he has ever seen.
Barbara Had a Brother. Nick tells his grandson Remie about Barbara, an old school friend. She used to be big and bad, but only because someone was being big and bad to her.
Honeycomb. The children are in the meadow. The Nogglins discover a land of hexagons, and everywhere looks the same. Itty leads the way, but can she find a route back?
Mud. Bella and Lift visit the Cloud Kitchen, where Patch is busy with the Playtimers making a tasty treat called Munchy Muddy Meadows.
Story. Bing and Coco have a disagreement over what story to read to Charlie.
Grass. Dee encourages a friend to say 'grass', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see green, spiky and hairy grass, and Dee finds some fairy grass!
I Can Catch. Lu isn't as good at catching as she thought she was.
A Door for Some Deer. The Piggies fix Fred and Dana's door but forget how to open it!
More About Cleaning. Stop-motion preschool programme. The Twirlywoos watch a cleaner cleaning a floor with a mop, but people with muddy shoes keep making the floor dirty again.
Outdoor Adventure. Mr Tumble, Justin and his friend all have some rollercoaster fun.
Close the Gate. Preschool series. The Teletubbies accidentally leave the Tiddlytubbies' gate open and some of them escape into Home Dome.
Shops. Nigel and Baby Bear encourage grown-ups and their babies to explore shopping-themed toys and objects, through discovery, play, song and story time. Everyone at home can join in too!
Cafe. Elsee joins her friend Fiadh at a local café for a game of I-spy.

programmes.show_name: New: What's in Your Plant Pot?
programmes.show_description: Potatoes. Potato farmer Luke shows the Fact Pack how potatoes grow underground.
Hide-and-Seek. Murphi is so good at hiding that Rebecca decides to make a cardboard tube tracker dog. The Recycling Express brings a shoebox, which becomes home to a magnificent monster.
Radishraptor Rumpus. Ginger and the Peas encounter a pack of Radishraptors who steal their delicious pop drops!
Bumpy and the Wise Old Wolfhound. Bingo is sick in hospital, so Bluey and Dad send her a homemade video to lift her spirits.
The Training Badge. The Squirrels need some training to improve their concentration.

programmes.show_name: Hey Duggee: Top of the Pups
programmes.show_description: Duggee on the Dancefloor. Step to the left and jump to the beat, you better clap your hands and shuffle your feet - it's Duggee on the dance floor.
Wind Sounds and Sandcastles Walk. Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers make wind sounds and find a mermaid's purse on a walk to make sandcastles.
Meet George and Freddie's Family. George and Freddie set up an obstacle course for Mum and Dad. Can they do it blindfolded?
Valentina's Greek Vegetarian Moussaka. Valentina shows us how to make a tasty Greek meal. Her moussaka recipe is loaded with fresh vegetables and topped with green lentils.
Shipping Container and Bubble Packaging. Maddie learns how shipping containers are moved around and how bubble packaging is made.
Mission: Toy Shop. Can disability dog Tinks use her superfetch skills to find Charli's phone at the toy shop?
Cassowary and Gharial. Steve and Aneeshwar meet the dinosaur-like cassowary and the amazing Sunda gharial.
Training Waffle. Preschool comedy drama series. Evie, Doug, Jess and Gramps take Waffle to his first puppy training class. Waffle can't resist causing chaos.
Three's a Crowd. Baba, Pip and Pop spend the day together, but Pip keeps getting left out!
Small Potatoes' Waltz Dancing. Animated series about a group of singing potatoes. The waltz is as easy as one, two, three when the Small Potatoes travel to Victorian-era Vienna!
Chrysalis Creche. TapTap becomes very protective when strange eggs appear in the branches of her tree, so everyone helps to keep the garden nice and quiet.
Echoes. Maddie and Triggs are magically transported to a world of echoes inside the belly of a whale, where they try to help their new friend Harriot rediscover her singing voice.
The Favourite Badge. The Squirrels learn what a favourite is but can't decide on their own choices.
Subtraction Somersaults. Thirteen is learning to jump and take away some blocks. What's left?
R's Really Rare Rose. Alphablock R's really rare rose is wilting. Can Alphablock W save the day?
Let's Pretend. Justin and his friend play pizza restaurants, and Mr Tumble knits a jumper.
Tickly. Preschool series. The Teletubbies enjoy doing Round and Round the Garden, especially the end of the rhyme where they all tickle each other.
Hedges. Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on an eco quest to find out all about hedges.
Bear Rescue. Taran has lost his favourite teddy, can the Fantastics find it before it's too late?
Racing Machines. Series for young children that celebrates many different kinds of amazing machines. In this episode, Grace shows off three incredible machines that are all designed to race.
Chalicothere and Claw. Preschool adventure series. Andy breaks a valuable museum artefact and has to travel back in time 30 million years to replace it.
Fluffytown Rocks Day. Love Monster loses his lucky rock and worries that without it, he can't DJ.
Devil's Hole Pupfish. The Octonauts help pupfish in the Nevada Desert when their aquatic home is in trouble.
The Tale of Old Brown's Feather. Animated series. When Peter takes one of Old Brown's tail feathers, a wild chase across the valley ensues to retrieve it and pacify the furious owl!
The A Cappella Badge. The Squirrels are inspired to learn how to sing a cappella style after hearing a famous bird quartet singing in a nearby tree.
Popcorn Gets Wheel Tired. After Popcorn runs on his hamster wheel all night, he's cranky the next day. Will he nap?
Poor Little Pea. Evil Pea plans to break the Tatoscope by pretending she's hurt her foot and getting Supertato to invite her into Tato Tower to recover.
Mission Mini. It's mini mayhem when the ducklings find a device that can shrink things.
Mojo Rocks. A rockslide blocks Ted the bus and his passengers in a mountain pass. Mojo's mighty dozer top will make short work of those rocks... if Mojo and Bo can work together!
Cricket. During a friendly game of neighbourhood cricket, the dads struggle to bowl Rusty out.
The Merguin. The Piripenguins fall under the spell of a magical Merguin, a mermaid-penguin, who has the ability to change their personality. But all is not as it seems.
Flat-Pack. While Mum and Dad struggle to build new flat-pack furniture, Bluey and Bingo transform the empty boxes into their own new world.
Andy and the Mischievous Dino. Andy embarks on an epic adventure to uncover the identity of a mysterious dinosaur.
Waffle Make and Do. Doug helps Waffle make new neighbour Pepper a dog bed. They fondly remember when Mrs Hobbs adopted Pepper and when they made their first box-bed for her cat, George.
The Freeze Wish. Rafi freezes the café to read with Finn, but mischief follows. She decides a quiet bedroom corner is the perfect place to read books.
It's Time to Build a Bug Hotel. JoJo, Gran Gran and Cynthia build a bug hotel and welcome some new residents.
Boo Night. Animation for preschool children. Sarah and Duck learn all about Boo Night and attempt to make their own scary costumes.
Major's Low-Gravity Backpack. Animation about a family of small creatures who live on, and inside, a planet. The Clangers need to clean the dustweeds from the ceiling of their cave.
A Gift from the Heart. MeMo and BaBa try to create a surprise gift for Gramma Leeba, but something always goes wrong. Gramma Leeba reassures them that the best gift is made with love.
Where's Upsy Daisy Gone? Series for young children. Upsy Daisy teaches Igglepiggle how to play hiding. Upsy Daisy is very good at hiding. Igglepiggle is impressed.
Tom Hardy - The Oak Tree. Tom Hardy tells the epic story of a tiny acorn's thousand-year journey, from seedling to mighty oak tree.
